_metadata.name: tx-resources-v0.1
_metadata.description: Curated Texas reentry resources for the Pathways navigator. Covers housing, employment, legal aid, benefits enrollment, recovery support, and crisis lines. Each entry is verifiable against the org's public-facing website at last_verified.
_metadata.scope: Texas, with regional focus on DFW and Greater Houston
_metadata.disclaimer: Resources are provided for navigation purposes. Availability, hours, eligibility, and contact info change. Pathways' production deployment refreshes this data weekly via a scheduled job; this seed file is a starting set.

resources.name: Texas RioGrande Legal Aid
resources.description: Free civil legal services to low-income people across 68 Texas counties. Handles housing, family, public benefits, consumer, and reentry-related civil matters. Does not handle criminal cases.

resources.name: Lone Star Legal Aid
resources.description: Free civil legal aid for low-income, elderly, and military residents of 72 counties in Texas including Harris County (Houston) and the Gulf Coast. Provides Veterans' Legal Initiative and disaster response services.
resources.eligibility: Income-restricted; veteran status, age, or domestic violence may expand eligibility

resources.name: Texas Legal Services Center
resources.description: Statewide nonprofit that operates legal hotlines, including the Veterans Legal Assistance Hotline and Crime Victim Compensation. Self-help legal forms and information at TexasLawHelp.org.
resources.eligibility: Varies by hotline; many are open to all Texans

resources.name: TDCJ Reentry and Integration Division
resources.description: Texas Department of Criminal Justice Reentry and Integration Division coordinates reentry planning for releasing TDCJ offenders. Provides ID issuance support, transition assistance, and referrals.

resources.name: Texas Workforce Commission — Reentry Employment Services
resources.description: TWC connects justice-involved Texans to job training, on-the-job training subsidies, and fair-chance employer matching through local Workforce Solutions offices. Offers Fidelity Bonding Program coverage.

resources.name: Texas 211
resources.description: Free statewide information and referral hotline for health and human services. Available 24/7. Multilingual.

resources.name: Texas HHSC — SNAP / Your Texas Benefits
resources.description: Texas Health and Human Services Commission administers SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, and CHIP. Apply online at YourTexasBenefits.com or in person at a local HHSC office. Texas opted out of the federal lifetime SNAP ban for drug felonies in 2015.

resources.name: Texas Department of Public Safety — ID Recovery Program
resources.description: Texas DPS issues state identification cards. Returning citizens released from TDCJ are typically issued an ID upon release; replacement requires proof of identity documents (birth certificate, Social Security card).

resources.name: Social Security Administration — Houston field offices
resources.description: Replacement Social Security cards can be requested in person at any SSA field office with appropriate identity documents. Online replacement is available for some users via my Social Security account.

resources.name: Goodwill Industries of Houston — Workforce Programs
resources.description: Job training, GED prep, soft-skills coaching, and direct hire opportunities in retail and logistics. Operates a dedicated reentry workforce program serving Greater Houston.

resources.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
resources.description: 24/7 confidential support for survivors of domestic violence and their loved ones. Phone, text, and online chat available. Anonymous service.

resources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
resources.description: 24/7 free and confidential support for people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Press 1 for the Veterans Crisis Line, press 3 for LGBTQ+ support. Available via call, text, or chat.

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: 24/7, free, confidential treatment referral and information for individuals and families facing mental health and/or substance use disorders.

resources.name: Texas Veterans Commission
resources.description: State agency providing veterans claims assistance, education program support, employment services through Workforce Solutions, and the Fund for Veterans' Assistance grant program. Serves justice-involved veterans through TVC's Veterans Justice Outreach coordination.

resources.name: The Salvation Army Greater Houston Area Command
resources.description: Emergency shelter, transitional housing, addiction recovery (Adult Rehabilitation Center), and meal services. The Harbor Light Center provides intensive substance use recovery.

resources.name: Texas Center for Justice and Equity
resources.description: Statewide nonprofit working on smart justice reform, reentry, and policy advocacy. Connects directly impacted people and families with peer navigators and policy education.

resources.name: Harris County Public Defender's Office — Mental Health Division
resources.description: Court-appointed representation for indigent defendants in Harris County. Mental Health Division handles competency, civil commitment, and mental-health-related criminal matters.

resources.name: Texas Fair Defense Project
resources.description: Statewide nonprofit focused on fines and fees, debtors' prison practices, and bail reform. Operates the TX Driver's License Restoration Project that helps Texans whose licenses were suspended due to unpaid fines.
